_meta.note: Comparator labels are external reference labels only. They are NEVER BN training targets.
_meta.evaluationRule: mixed: entropy-aware v1.0 (9/59 stations, strict T1 subset) + v4.0 MAP (50/59 stations, non-T1)
_meta.note_externalSites: externalSites now distinguish editorial-only records from BN-descriptive site-level cases. External outputs remain site-level only, non-pooled, and not benchmark-comparable.
_meta.externalSitesNote: Meaningful partial BN-domain overlap can support descriptive BN site-level semantics for selected non-training sites. No pooled agreement statistics or benchmark-comparable claims are authorized in this tranche.

externalComparisonClassDefinitions.editorial_only: Editorial or descriptive reference content only. No BN-side external inference/comparison claim is being made.
externalComparisonClassDefinitions.bn_descriptive_site_level: Meaningful but partial BN-domain overlap supports descriptive BN-oriented site-level comparison only. Not pooled and not benchmark-comparable.
externalComparisonClassDefinitions.bn_evaluative_not_pooled: External BN-oriented comparison is evaluative, but not authorized for pooled agreement statistics or benchmark-comparable interpretation.

externalInterpretationRules.interpretationNote: External-site BN semantics are authorized for descriptive or evaluative-but-not-pooled use only. Missing trained contaminants, missing modifiers, and noncanonical contaminants must be represented explicitly as uncertainty.

comparisonClassDefinitions.causal_chain_complete: Comparator stations with the strongest evidentiary alignment to the BN-RRM causal chain; strongest interpretive weight.
comparisonClassDefinitions.partial_deterministic: Valid BN-vs-deterministic comparison context with weaker or incomplete evidentiary alignment; not equivalent to causal_chain_complete interpretation.
comparisonClassDefinitions.descriptive_only: Contextual comparison only; excluded from pooled agreement interpretation.

evaluationViewDefinitions.strict_t1_entropy_publication: Strict Tier 1 publication subset evaluated under the entropy-aware v1.0 rule.
evaluationViewDefinitions.broader_map_development: Broader development/canonical comparison context evaluated under MAP.
evaluationViewDefinitions.descriptive_only: Descriptive-only comparison context without pooled agreement interpretation.

pooledStatsPolicy.interpretation_note: Pooled agreement statistics combine causal_chain_complete and partial_deterministic rows and are not equivalent to a causal_chain_complete-only benchmark.

low.justification: Direct ordinal alignment: WOE low = BN low (both indicate minimal ecological concern)

negligible.justification: WOE negligible maps to BN low per governance spec Section 7.2 (both indicate no significant risk)

moderate.justification: Direct ordinal alignment: WOE moderate = BN moderate

moderate-to-high.justification: Conservative mapping per governance spec Section 7.2: WOE moderate-to-high maps to BN high

high.justification: Direct ordinal alignment: WOE high = BN high

externalSites.siteName: Westminster Pier Park, 224 Front Street, New Westminster
externalSites.gateReason: Class 2 descriptive integrated comparison. Genuine Aquatic DERA with WOE approach integrating multiple LOEs per AEC. Area-level conclusions (per-AEC: AEC 1B, AEC 22A/B, AEC 2/16B/17A/B). No station-level risk classifications. Sediment + groundwater + porewater assessed. Fraser River foreshore (tidally influenced).
externalSites.reportTitle: Human Health and Detailed Ecological Risk Assessment, 224 Front Street, New Westminster, BC
externalSites.uncertaintyNote: The 0139 v1.1 BN run uses an AEC 1B descriptive profile with chemistry, TOC, PCBs (below_isqg, site-wide non-detect), and tox_amphipod (moderate, CETIS direct numeric). Assessment mode is used to condition on the observed H. azteca toxicity. grain_size and sulfide_binding remain missing; taxa_richness and diversity are interpretation-only. The posterior is descriptive site-level context only and is not pooled, benchmark-comparable, or validation evidence. MAP flipped from low (v1) to moderate (v1.1) on addition of observed tox_amphipod=moderate as hard evidence.

bnInputCoverage.note: AEC 1B chemistry nodes populated from explicit SED10-49 and SED10-51 values (Appendix 2 Tables 2-58 and 2-63). TOC from Table 2-55. PCBs encoded as below_isqg: site-wide non-detect program confirmed (v1.1). tox_amphipod encoded as moderate: CETIS 19-3393-7598/10203a numeric H. azteca survival, assessment mode (v1.1). grain_size, sulfide_binding remain missing. taxa_richness, diversity remain interpretation-only.

loes: Multiple LOE within each AEC (sediment chemistry, groundwater/porewater chemistry, toxicity testing)
Engineering controls and anticipated post-remediation improvements
reportConclusions.woeIntegrationNote: WOE approach integrating multiple LOE per AEC. Conclusion considers probability, magnitude and scale of harm, and ecological relevance. Engineering controls (Jet Grout containment wall, sediment capping) contribute to the low-risk determination.
reportConclusions.mappingJustification: WorleyParsons 'low overall risk' maps approximately to BN Low. Mapping is approximate because: (1) the WOE integrates remediation context (engineering controls) alongside ecological evidence, making it partially a managed-risk conclusion; (2) the conclusion is site-wide, not per-station; (3) the Fraser River foreshore is estuarine/tidal, overlapping with but not identical to the BN-RRM marine training domain.

provenance.sourceDocument: WorleyParsons Canada Services Ltd. (2011). Human Health and Detailed Ecological Risk Assessment, 224 Front Street, New Westminster, BC. Project 09216, Rev 0.
provenance.sourceTableFigure: Executive Summary, Aquatic DERA section (p. vi)
provenance.extractedLabel: Low overall risk to aquatic ROPCs

loes: Sediment and groundwater chemistry + toxicity testing within AEC 1B
reportConclusions.mappingJustification: Per-AEC conclusion from WOE integration. AEC 1B is the primary near-field assessment area. Mapping is approximate — AEC is an area-level unit, not station-level.

provenance.sourceDocument: WorleyParsons (2011). HHERA, 224 Front Street, New Westminster, BC.

loes: Sediment and groundwater chemistry within AEC 22A/B
reportConclusions.mappingJustification: Per-AEC conclusion. AEC 22A/B is an area-level assessment unit covering a defined portion of the foreshore.

provenance.sourceDocument: WorleyParsons (2011). HHERA, 224 Front Street, New Westminster, BC.

statisticalAuthorization.reason: A governed AEC 1B descriptive BN run is now available for 0139, but it remains site-level only, non-pooled, and non-benchmark-comparable. Consultant WOE conclusions are still external reference labels and no station-level paired labels exist for agreement statistics.

taxa_richness.reason: Community metrics were not sourced as existing-node numeric inputs.

diversity.reason: Community metrics were not sourced as existing-node numeric inputs.

aecContext.sourceTableFigure: Appendix 17 Table B Overview of AEC 1B
aecContext.note: AEC 1B sediment samples within the ecologically active zone are SED10-49 and SED10-51. These are the authorized AEC 1B chemistry samples for the descriptive BN run.

toxicity.sourceTableFigure: Appendix 17 Table F Summary of Toxicity Test Effects Levels
toxicity.note: H. azteca (tox_amphipod) promoted to direct encoding in v1.1 — see aec1bToxicology.hyalellaAzteca. C. tentans data documented in aec1bToxicology.chironomusTentans; remains interpretation-only pending future tox_chironomid DAG node.

community.sourceTableFigure: Table Y Summary of Management Goals, Assessment and Measurement Endpoints and Risk Hypotheses for the Aquatic DERA
community.note: Community evidence remains qualitative and interpretation-only for this MVP.

bnRunRecord.runMethod: Existing v1.0 assessment inference via fit_causal_model.expand_organic_for_inference -> _build_evidence(mode='assessment') -> _build_soft_evidence -> forward_inference using learned-model.json. Assessment mode used in v1.1+ because tox_amphipod is a direct CETIS numeric observation (not interpretation-only).
bnRunRecord.evidenceSourceSummary: Explicit AEC 1B sediment values from Appendix 2 Tables 2-55, 2-58, and 2-63 for SED10-49 and SED10-51, with AEC 1B sample scope confirmed in Appendix 17 Table B.
bnRunRecord.encodingBasis: Chemistry nodes populated from explicit AEC 1B sample values using existing discretizers. TOC populated directly. sed_pcbs encoded below_isqg from site-wide PCB non-detect program (v1.1). tox_amphipod encoded moderate from CETIS 19-3393-7598/10203a H. azteca survival (v1.1, assessment mode). grain_size and sulfide_binding remain missing. taxa_richness and diversity remain interpretation-only.
limitations: This intake is AEC 1B-specific and not a whole-site Westminster rollup.

sed_pcbs encoded as below_isqg in v1.1: site-wide PCB program confirmed all sediment samples tested including SED10-49 and SED10-51; no detections. Specific MDL and table reference to be added from PCB analytical table.
grain_size remains missing pending review of sieve analysis data in the analytical tables (user searching; may be resolved in v1.2).

sulfide_binding intentionally left unobserved: metals show only one minor exceedance (Pb, minor), PCBs non-detect site-wide, and the RA downgraded overall effects to low post-remediation. SEM/AVS measurement would not materially change the risk inference given the low metal contamination profile. Marginalization over sulfide_binding states is the appropriate treatment.
tox_amphipod promoted from interpretation-only to direct encoding in v1.1 using CETIS 19-3393-7598/10203a numeric survival rates.

tox_chironomid does not exist as a BN node; C. tentans data (SED10-49: 67.5% significant p<0.0001; SED10-51: 82% significant p=0.022) is documented in aec1bToxicology.chironomusTentans for future DAG expansion.
taxa_richness and diversity remain interpretation-only; community evidence is qualitative.
The posterior is descriptive-only and must not be interpreted as validation, pooled agreement, or benchmark comparability.
Organic inference uses existing v4.1 soft-evidence handling for PAH-only organic evidence with sed_pcbs left unobserved.

changeFromPriorRun.changeSummary: MAP flipped low -> moderate. Primary driver: tox_amphipod=moderate (CETIS 19-3393-7598/10203a, H. azteca 74% survival p=0.030, SED10-49) is now injected as hard evidence via assessment mode. In v1, screening mode discarded the observation entirely. Secondary: sed_pcbs promoted from missing to below_isqg (site-wide non-detect); this had negligible effect because the soft-evidence organic handler uses derived organic states, not raw PCB evidence. The posterior shift is fully attributable to assessment-mode inclusion of observed toxicity.

externalSites.gateReason: WOE conclusions are site-level per receptor group, not station-level. PAH-only contamination profile (no metals COPCs). Cannot support station-level agreement statistics.
externalSites.reportTitle: Detailed Risk Assessment, Water Lot Portion of 1 Port Drive, Nanaimo, BC
externalSites.uncertaintyNote: Water Lot, 1 Port Drive has meaningful but PAH-dominant BN-domain overlap supported by toxicity evidence. Missing metals, modifier, and community inputs limit interpretation to descriptive site-level BN context only.

bnInputCoverage.note: PAH-only contamination profile. 7 of 9 substance nodes (metals) have no site-specific evidence. BN inference is partial-evidence.

reportConclusions.woeIntegrationNote: Moderate chemistry hazard refuted by higher-weighted negligible toxicity and tissue LOEs.
reportConclusions.mappingJustification: Tetra Tech Negligible maps approximately to BN Low — both indicate minimal ecological concern. Mapping is approximate because the frameworks use different conceptual bases and the WOE scale is a 3-level hazard scale, not a 3-class ecological risk scale.

provenance.sourceDocument: Tetra Tech EBA Inc. (2015). Detailed Risk Assessment, Water Lot Portion of 1 Port Drive, Nanaimo, BC. File: ENVIND03511-02.

reportConclusions.mappingJustification: Aquatic macrophytes are not a BN-RRM receptor group. No mapping to 3-class ecological risk.

provenance.sourceDocument: Tetra Tech EBA Inc. (2015). Detailed Risk Assessment, Water Lot Portion of 1 Port Drive, Nanaimo, BC.

reportConclusions.mappingJustification: Fish risk derived from benthic results, not independently assessed. Not directly comparable to BN-RRM ecological_risk.

provenance.sourceDocument: Tetra Tech EBA Inc. (2015). Detailed Risk Assessment, Water Lot Portion of 1 Port Drive, Nanaimo, BC.

statisticalAuthorization.reason: No station-level paired labels. WOE conclusions are site-level per receptor. Only descriptive comparison authorized.

externalSites.siteName: Chevron Burnaby Refinery, Seep Area Foreshore
externalSites.gateReason: Class 3 partial-method comparison. HQ-based area-level ecological conclusions (Eastern Seep vs Western Seep). No WOE or LoE integration framework applied. Petroleum-dominant contamination profile (LEPH, VPH, benzene, ethylbenzene, xylenes) with minimal BN-RRM substance overlap (Cu, Zn only in porewater/prey pathway). Cannot support station-level agreement statistics or multi-LoE descriptive integrated comparison.
externalSites.reportTitle: Human Health and Ecological Risk Assessment of Seep Area, Foreshore Down Slope of the East Impounding Basin, Chevron Burnaby Refinery, Burnaby, BC
externalSites.uncertaintyNote: Chevron Burnaby Refinery Seep Area is petroleum-dominant and falls largely outside the trained BN contaminant domain. Keep this entry editorial/descriptive only; BN-side external comparison is not currently supported.

bnInputCoverage.note: Petroleum-dominant profile (LEPH, VPH, BTEX). Sediment COPCs are LEPH, VPH, benzene, ethylbenzene, xylenes — none are BN-RRM canonical substance nodes. Cu and Zn appear only as porewater/prey-pathway COPCs, not sediment COPCs. No sediment metals, PAHs (as Total PAHs), PCBs, TOC, grain size, or AVS reported as COPCs. Benthic invertebrate sampling conducted (SLR field program) but not as formal toxicity testing or community survey — used as supporting line of evidence only. BN-RRM inference would rely almost entirely on prior distributions.

loes: HQ Screening: LEPHw in porewater (HQ 3.76-5.92), benzo(a)pyrene in porewater (HQ evaluated), zinc in porewater (HQ 1.12)
reportConclusions.woeIntegrationNote: HQ > 1 for LEPHw and zinc in porewater, but risk considered low based on limited area of impact, intermittent tidal submergence, lower habitat suitability of mid/upper intertidal seep area, and low magnitude of HQs. Not a WOE integration — professional judgment applied to HQ results with supporting site observations.
reportConclusions.mappingJustification: SLR 'low risk (acceptable)' maps weakly to BN Low. The consultant conclusion is based on HQ magnitude + area-of-impact argument, not an integrated multi-LoE framework. The mapping is weak because: (1) HQ screening is a single-pathway method, not comparable to multi-LoE ecological risk classification; (2) the petroleum COPCs do not overlap with BN-RRM substance nodes; (3) the 'acceptable' determination is partially based on remedial context (IRA barriers in place), not solely on ecological risk level.

provenance.sourceDocument: SLR Consulting (Canada) Ltd. (2014). HHERA of Seep Area, Foreshore Down Slope of the East Impounding Basin, Chevron Burnaby Refinery. SLR Project No. 201.00959.00001.
provenance.extractedLabel: Low risk (acceptable) — HQs for LEPHw and zinc > 1 but considered low based on limited impact area and low magnitude

loes: HQ Screening: LEPH, VPH, benzene, ethylbenzene, xylenes in sediment (Pre-IRA barriers scenario only — contaminated sediment removed during IRA installation)
reportConclusions.woeIntegrationNote: Sediment COPCs (LEPH, VPH, xylenes) were identified under the pre-IRA scenario only. The contaminated sediment has been removed during IRA barrier installation. Risk from sediment COPCs is not a concern under the current IRA barriers scenario.
reportConclusions.mappingJustification: Pre-IRA scenario only. Contaminated sediment has been removed. No current sediment risk to map. The BN-RRM models current ecological risk, not historical pre-remediation conditions.

provenance.sourceDocument: SLR Consulting (Canada) Ltd. (2014). HHERA of Seep Area, Foreshore Down Slope of the East Impounding Basin, Chevron Burnaby Refinery.
provenance.sourceTableFigure: Section 6.3.1 and Executive Summary Table (p. iii)
provenance.extractedLabel: Sediment COPCs not a concern under current IRA barriers exposure scenario

loes: HQ Screening: Dietary exposure via prey (benzo(a)pyrene, copper, zinc). All HQs < 1.
reportConclusions.mappingJustification: Birds and mammals are not directly modeled by BN-RRM ecological_risk node. Not mappable.

provenance.sourceDocument: SLR Consulting (Canada) Ltd. (2014). HHERA of Seep Area, Foreshore Down Slope of the East Impounding Basin, Chevron Burnaby Refinery.
provenance.extractedLabel: Negligible — all HQs < 1 for all scenarios

statisticalAuthorization.reason: Class 3 partial-method only. No station-level labels. No multi-LoE integration. HQ screening is a single-pathway method. Petroleum-dominant COPCs do not overlap with BN-RRM substance nodes. Only narrative partial-method comparison authorized: BN-RRM posterior vs consultant HQ-based area-level conclusion.
